contentBlocks.original:
धरणीगर्भ सम्भूतं विद्युक्यान्तिसमप्रभम् ।
कुमारं शक्ति हस्तं तं मङ्गलं प्रणमाम्यहम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dharaṇīgarbha sambhūtaṃ vidyukyāntisamaprabham ।
kumāraṃ śakti hastaṃ taṃ maṅgaḻaṃ praṇamāmyaham ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Maṅgala, born from the womb of the earth, radiant as lightning, the young prince, holding the śakti-spear in hand. (1)

contentBlocks.original:
महीसुत महाभागो मङ्गलो मङ्गलप्रदः ।
महावीरो महाशूरो महाबल पराक्रमः ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahīsuta mahābhāgō maṅgaḻō maṅgaḻapradaḥ ।
mahāvīrō mahāśūrō mahābala parākramaḥ ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Son of the earth, of great fortune, Maṅgala, bestower of auspiciousness; great hero, great warrior, of mighty strength and prowess — (2)

contentBlocks.original:
भरध्वाज कुलोद्भूतो भूसुतो भव्य भूषणः ।
मेरुं प्रदक्षिणं कृत्वा सर्वदेवात्म सिद्दिदम् ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bharadhvāja kulōdbhūtō bhūsutō bhavya bhūṣaṇaḥ ।
mēruṃ pradakṣiṇaṃ kṛtvā sarvadēvātma siddidam ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Born from the lineage of Bharadvāja, the earth-son, of handsome adornment; having circumambulated Mount Meru, the giver of all divine accomplishments — (3)

contentBlocks.original:
नमस्ते महाशक्ति पाणे नमस्ते लसद्वज्रपाणे ।
नमस्ते कटिन्यस्तपाणे नमस्ते सदाभीष्टपाणे ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namastē mahāśakti pāṇē namastē lasadvajrapāṇē ।
namastē kaṭinyastapāṇē namastē sadābhīṣṭapāṇē ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to you holding the great spear; salutation to you bearing the gleaming thunderbolt; salutation to you of firm and mighty hands; salutation to you whose hands ever grant all desired things. (4)

contentBlocks.original:
चतुर्भुजां मेषवाहनं वरदां वसुधाप्रियम् ।
रत्तमाल्याम्बरधरं तं अङ्गारकं प्रणमाम्यहम् ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chaturbhujāṃ mēṣavāhanaṃ varadāṃ vasudhāpriyam ।
rattamālyāmbaradharaṃ taṃ aṅgārakaṃ praṇamāmyaham ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Aṅgāraka, four-armed, riding a ram, bestowing boons, beloved of the earth, wearing red garlands and red robes. (5)
